commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs human review for compatibility/default approval plus contributor-supplied video proof; there is no narrow automation repair lane while the remaining blocker is proof and maintainer judgment.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain issue was found; the PR does not add dependencies, lockfile changes, broad permissions, secret handling, or third-party code execution beyond existing provider HTTP paths. (sha=f1f08f7213cd7d780552be83859d61514077d0e7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the bundled DeepInfra provider to use auth-aware live model catalogs, adds image/video catalog registration, changes DeepInfra defaults/media wiring, and adds Anthropic cache-control wrapping plus docs/tests.

PR surface: Source +698, Tests +879, Docs +5, Other 0. Total +1582 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main returns only configured DeepInfra augment entries and uses the OpenRouter cache wrapper despite DeepInfra anthropic cache eligibility; I did not run live DeepInfra credentials in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs human review for compatibility/default approval plus contributor-supplied video proof; there is no narrow automation repair lane while the remaining blocker is proof and maintainer judgment.

target.title: fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse t…
target.body: # fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse them during onboarding ## Summary - **Problem**: DeepInfra plugin pinned the chat catalog to 7 hardcoded manifest entries (a wizard-vs-discovery guard short-circuited discovery once the auth wizard seeded models), had no live discovery seam for image-gen / video-gen, and silently dropped Anthropic `cache_control` markers when proxying `anthropic/*` models — prompt caching was advertised eligible but never fired. - **Solution**: Wire DeepInfra's `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` (the agent-tagged projection now shipping in the backend) through a per-surface helper that buckets responses by short-alias tag (`chat`/`vlm`/`embed`/`image-gen`/`video-gen`/`tts`/`stt`). Register live image-gen and video-gen catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern). Replace the OpenRouter-only Anthropic cache wrapper with a deepinfra-local one gated on the model id. - **What changed**: new `surface-model-catalogs.ts` (live image-gen + video-gen catalog providers + `resolveModelCapabilities`); new `cache-wrapper.ts` (Anthropic ephemeral cache marker injection for `anthropic/*` on DeepInfra); refactored `provider-models.ts` (per-surface bucketed discovery, API-key gated, 5-min cache, sync static fallback); media-provider builders now accept the per-surface catalog; `index.ts` feeds all media surfaces from one source + registers the new catalog providers; `media-models.ts` `DEFAULT_*` constants demoted to `*_FALLBACK_MODELS`. - **Scope boundary**: No edits outside `extensions/deepinfra/`. No SDK changes (`src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts` is byte-identical to `main`). TTS/STT/VLM/embed surfaces still use static fallback only — extending `UnifiedModelCatalogKind` to cover them is a separate SDK PR. ## Motivation DeepInfra ships hundreds of models, but openclaw only surfaced 7 hardcoded chat IDs — every new model needed a code change here. The DeepInfra backend now emits surface-tagged responses on `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta`, so the plugin can map tags → surfaces dynamically. Anthropic prompt caching on DeepInfra was also silently broken: the runner advertised eligibility but the OpenRouter-only stream wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so `cache_control` markers never went out. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix ## Scope - [x] Integrations ## Linked Issue/PR - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** DeepInfra plugin: (1) discovers the full live chat catalog (88 models, vs 7 hardcoded on `main`) — wizard-vs-discovery guard no longer suppresses dynamic results; (2) registers live `image-gen` and `video-gen` catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern); (3) injects Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` markers on outgoing payloads for `deepinfra/anthropic/*` requests (OpenRouter wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"` before this patch); (4) wires TTS / STT / image-gen / VLM end-to-end through the deepinfra speech, media-understanding, image-gen, and VLM providers. **Real environment tested:** Hosted Lightsail VM (Ubuntu 24.04, Node 24.15.0), openclaw `2026.5.21` built from this PR, `openclaw.service` running as a systemd unit. Live network to `api.deepinfra.com`. DeepInfra key configured. **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Capability inventory commands (chat catalog + image + audio providers): ```bash pnpm dev --dev capability model providers | grep deepinfra pnpm dev --dev capability image providers | grep deepinfra pnpm dev --dev capability audio providers | grep deepinfra ``` Then in the webchat UI: image-gen → VLM → STT (inbound audio attachment auto-transcribed) → TTS in one chat session (full session log attached below). Anthropic cache markers — HTTP-level capture via a small Node logging proxy in front of `api.deepinfra.com`; agent fires one `anthropic/*` turn, proxy records the outgoing body: ```bash openclaw agent --local --model deepinfra/anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 \ --message "Say hello briefly." < /dev/null cat /tmp/cache-proxy.log ``` Test suite: ```bash pnpm test extensions/deepinfra pnpm check:changed ``` **Evidence after fix:** Terminal — `capability` commands show 88 dynamically-discovered chat models, image providers configured, audio providers configured: Onboard summary — default model auto-selected from discovery: Model picker — full discovered DeepInfra catalog (Anthropic, DeepSeek, Google, Meta, MoonshotAI, NVIDIA, Qwen, ZAI, ...): Webchat session JSONL — image-gen → VLM → STT → TTS, all positive outcomes, redacted: [f26d402a-ee0a-4014-b916-6aa0579450b1.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172089/f26d402a-ee0a-4014-b916-6aa0579450b1.log) Generated image (1792×1024 JPEG produced by the `image_generate` tool): Passive STT — agent responds to the auto-transcribed inbound audio attachment: STT input: [bobbyanaya-...mp3](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172146/bobbyanaya-texas-prison-slang-three-greetings-same-meaning-whatx27s-up-165808.mp3) TTS — `tts` tool fires; the chat UI renders a playable audio bubble: TTS output: [voice-...mp3](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172138/voice-1779514789533---942686ed-8f59-4165-95ce-8e588eafff99.mp3) Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` marker captured on the outgoing HTTPS body to DeepInfra via a logging proxy — proves the deepinfra-local cache wrapper fires where the upstream OpenRouter wrapper would have short-circuited: **Observed result after fix:** Six surfaces work end-to-end on live deployment: dynamic chat catalog (88 models), live image-gen catalog (FLUX), live audio provider catalog, in-chat image generation produces an on-disk JPEG, in-chat passive STT transcribes inbound audio, in-chat TTS produces a playable MP3 attachment, and Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` markers reach DeepInfra's API on `anthropic/*` requests. `pnpm test extensions/deepinfra` → 69/69 green; `pnpm check:changed` lanes → exit 0. **What was not tested:** Long-form video-gen completion (the request fires successfully; the upstream model just doesn't finish within the `fetchWithSsrFGuard` 60 s window — separate upstream concern); the cache-marker test request itself returned an empty response from Anthropic (unrelated upstream model behavior), but the marker injection is independently captured at the wire by the proxy and shown above. ## Root Cause - (a) `augmentModelCatalog` short-circuited with `existingDeepInfraCount > DEEPINFRA_MODEL_CATALOG.length`, skipping discovery whenever the auth wizard had seeded entries first. - (b) Media surfaces had no `registerModelCatalogProvider` wiring. - (c) `wrapStreamFn` reused `createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper`, which short-circuits on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so Anthropic ephemeral cache markers never reached the payload for DeepInfra-routed Anthropic models even though `isCacheTtlEligible` returned `true`. **Missing guardrail**: no test asserted that `wrapStreamFn` applied `cache_control` markers for `anthropic/*` ids; no test asserted that the augmented catalog grew past the manifest size on successful discovery. ## Regression Test Plan - **Coverage level**: [x] Unit test [x] Seam / integration test - **Target tests**: - `extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts` (new) — asserts `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }` markers are applied for `anthropic/*` ids and skipped otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/surface-model-catalogs.test.ts` (new) — asserts the live catalog providers return `null` without a key, return `null` on empty surface buckets, and project entries with the correct shape otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts` (extended) — per-surface bucketing + manifest fallback path. - `extensions/deepinfra/index.test.ts` (extended) — augmentModelCatalog merges discovered + wizard-seeded entries instead of suppressing one. - extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts (4 tests) exercises createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper against anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 & anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 and asserts cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" } is injected into the system message under both string-content and array-content shapes; negative test confirms non-anthropic ids pass through untouched. - extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts (3 new cases) mocks openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.isProviderApiKeyConfigured and asserts hasDeepInfraApiKey({ env: {}, agentDir }) falls back to the auth-profile store with the correct provider/agentDir args, short-circuits on env var, and returns false when both env and store are empty. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - **Chat catalogue**: with `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` set, OpenClaw surfaces the full DeepInfra catalogue (88 chat + 38 vlm today across `anthropic/`, `ByteDance/`, `deepseek-ai/`, `google/`, `meta-llama/`, `Qwen/`, `zai-org/`, …). Without a key, the curated manifest fallback is served. - **Image-gen + video-gen**: a live `registerModelCatalogProvider` now refreshes per-kind catalogs from DeepInfra when a key is configured. Without a key, the curated static fallback is served. - **Onboarding picker**: discovered models are no longer hidden behind wizard-seeded entries. - **Anthropic prompt caching** actually fires on DeepInfra-routed `anthropic/*` requests. Multi-turn conversations should show non-zero `cache_creation_input_tokens` on turn 1 and `cache_read_input_tokens` on turn 2+. - **Default chat model**: users on the *implicit* DeepInfra default now route to `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash` (Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B has been retired from the active catalog). Anyone who pinned a model explicitly via config is unaffected. - Backward compatible at the config layer; no schema changes, no env var renames, no migration script. ### Auth-context threading fix `provider-discovery.ts` now passes the resolved catalog context (`env`, `agentDir`, and `hasApiKey=true` once `buildSingleProviderApiKeyCatalog` has verified the key resolves) through to `discoverDeepInfraModels`. Without this, an auth-profile-only setup could authenticate at the discovery layer yet still receive the static catalog because `hasDeepInfraApiKey` had no `agentDir` to probe the profile store. Both env-var and auth-profile-only setups now receive the live 88-model catalog identically. ## Diagram ```text Before: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds 7 manifest entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: count > MODEL_CATALOG.length? -> skip discovery] -> [picker shows 7 models] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper -> provider !== "openrouter" -> short-circuit] -> [payload sent WITHOUT cache_control markers, prompt cache never fires] After: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: always run discovery] -> [discoverDeepInfraSurfaces: GET /v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta] -> [bucket by surface alias tag] -> [picker shows 88 chat + 38 vlm + ...] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper: model.id starts with "anthropic/" -> apply patch] -> [payload sent WITH cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}, prompt cache fires] ``` ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **Yes** — one read-only GET to `https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` per process per 5 minutes, gated on `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` being configured. The endpoint is unauthenticated; the gate is a UX choice (pre-auth flows show the static fallback, no useless network traffic). No PII or secrets in the request. - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Risks and Mitigations - **Risk**: production DeepInfra backend hasn't (yet) tagged any `video-gen` model with the OPENAI tag, so the `video-gen` surface bucket from live discovery is empty. **Mitigation**: the live catalog seam returns `null` on empty buckets so the static `DEEPINFRA_VIDEO_FALLBACK_MODELS` list wins. Once the backend tags T2V models, the live catalog takes over automatically — no deepclaw redeploy needed.
target.author: ats3v

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

Without a key, the curated static fallback is served. - **Onboarding picker**: discovered models are no longer hidden behind wizard-seeded entries. - **Anthropic prompt caching** actually fires on DeepInfra-routed `anthropic/*` requests. Anthropic prompt caching on DeepInfra was also silently broken: the runner advertised eligibility but the OpenRouter-only stream wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so `cache_control` markers never went out. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix ## Scope - [x] Integrations ## Linked Issue/PR - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** DeepInfra plugin: (1) discovers the full live chat catalog (88 models, vs 7 hardcoded on `main`) — wizard-vs-discovery guard no longer suppresses dynamic results; (2) registers live `image-gen` and `video-gen` catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern); (3) injects Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` markers on outgoing payloads for `deepinfra/anthropic/*` requests (OpenRouter wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"` before this patch); (4) wires TTS / STT / image-gen / VLM end-to-end through the deepinfra speech, media-understanding, image-gen, and VLM providers. **Real environment tested:** Hosted Lightsail VM (Ubuntu 24.04, Node 24.15.0), openclaw `2026.5.21` built from this PR, `openclaw.service` running as a systemd unit. Live network to `api.deepinfra.com`. DeepInfra key configured. **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Capability inventory commands (chat catalog + image + audio providers): ```bash pnpm dev --dev capability model providers | grep deepinfra pnpm dev --dev capability image providers | grep deepinfra pnpm dev --dev capability audio providers | grep deepinfra ``` Then in the webchat UI: image-gen → VLM → STT (inbound audio attachment auto-transcribed) → TTS in one chat session (full session log attached below). Anthropic cache markers — HTTP-level capture via a small Node logging proxy in front of `api.deepinfra.com`; agent fires one `anthropic/*` turn, proxy records the outgoing body: ```bash openclaw agent --local --model deepinfra/anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 \ --message "Say hello briefly." < /dev/null cat /tmp/cache-proxy.log ``` Test suite: ```bash pnpm test extensions/deepinfra pnpm check:changed ``` **Evidence after fix:** Terminal — `capability` commands show 88 dynamically-discovered chat models, image providers configured, audio providers configured: Onboard summary — default model auto-selected from discovery: Model picker — full discovered DeepInfra catalog (Anthropic, DeepSeek, Google, Meta, MoonshotAI, NVIDIA, Qwen, ZAI, ...): Webchat session JSONL — image-gen → VLM → STT → TTS, all positive outcomes, redacted: [f26d402a-ee0a-4014-b916-6aa0579450b1.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172089/f26d402a-ee0a-4014-b916-6aa0579450b1.log) Generated image (1792×1024 JPEG produced by the `image_generate` tool): Passive STT — agent responds to the auto-transcribed inbound audio attachment: STT input: [bobbyanaya-...mp3](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172146/bobbyanaya-texas-prison-slang-three-greetings-same-meaning-whatx27s-up-165808.mp3) TTS — `tts` tool fires; the chat UI renders a playable audio bubble: TTS output: [voice-...mp3](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28172138/voice-1779514789533---942686ed-8f59-4165-95ce-8e588eafff99.mp3) Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` marker captured on the outgoing HTTPS body to DeepInfra via a logging proxy — proves the deepinfra-local cache wrapper fires where the upstream OpenRouter wrapper would have short-circuited: **Observed result after fix:** Six surfaces work end-to-end on live deployment: dynamic chat catalog (88 models), live image-gen catalog (FLUX), live audio provider catalog, in-chat image generation produces an on-disk JPEG, in-chat passive STT transcribes inbound audio, in-chat TTS produces a playable MP3 attachment, and Anthropic ephemeral `cache_control` markers reach DeepInfra's API on `anthropic/*` requests. `pnpm test extensions/deepinfra` → 69/69 green; `pnpm check:changed` lanes → exit 0. **What was not tested:** Long-form video-gen completion (the request fires successfully; the upstream model just doesn't finish within the `fetchWithSsrFGuard` 60 s window — separate upstream concern); the cache-marker test request itself returned an empty response from Anthropic (unrelated upstream model behavior), but the marker injection is independently captured at the wire by the proxy and shown above. ## Root Cause - (a) `augmentModelCatalog` short-circuited with `existingDeepInfraCount > DEEPINFRA_MODEL_CATALOG.length`, skipping discovery whenever the auth wizard had seeded entries first. - (b) Media surfaces had no `registerModelCatalogProvider` wiring. - (c) `wrapStreamFn` reused `createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper`, which short-circuits on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so Anthropic ephemeral cache markers never reached the payload for DeepInfra-routed Anthropic models even though `isCacheTtlEligible` returned `true`. **Missing guardrail**: no test asserted that `wrapStreamFn` applied `cache_control` markers for `anthropic/*` ids; no test asserted that the augmented catalog grew past the manifest size on successful discovery. ## Regression Test Plan - **Coverage level**: [x] Unit test [x] Seam / integration test - **Target tests**: - `extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts` (new) — asserts `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }` markers are applied for `anthropic/*` ids and skipped otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/surface-model-catalogs.test.ts` (new) — asserts the live catalog providers return `null` without a key, return `null` on empty surface buckets, and project entries with the correct shape otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts` (extended) — per-surface bucketing + manifest fallback path. - `extensions/deepinfra/index.test.ts` (extended) — augmentModelCatalog merges discovered + wizard-seeded entries instead of suppressing one. - extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts (4 tests) exercises createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper against anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 & anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 and asserts cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" } is injected into the system message under both string-content and array-content shapes; negative test confirms non-anthropic ids pass through untouched. - extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts (3 new cases) mocks openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.isProviderApiKeyConfigured and asserts hasDeepInfraApiKey({ env: {}, agentDir }) falls back to the auth-profile store with the correct provider/agentDir args, short-circuits on env var, and returns false when both env and store are empty. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - **Chat catalogue**: with `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` set, OpenClaw surfaces the full DeepInfra catalogue (88 chat + 38 vlm today across `anthropic/`, `ByteDance/`, `deepseek-ai/`, `google/`, `meta-llama/`, `Qwen/`, `zai-org/`, …). Without a key, the curated manifest fallback is served. - **Image-gen + video-gen**: a live `registerModelCatalogProvider` now refreshes per-kind catalogs from DeepInfra when a key is configured. Without a key, the curated static fallback is served. - **Onboarding picker**: discovered models are no longer hidden behind wizard-seeded entries. - **Anthropic prompt caching** actually fires on DeepInfra-routed `anthropic/*` requests. Multi-turn conversations should show non-zero `cache_creation_input_tokens` on turn 1 and `cache_read_input_tokens` on turn 2+. - **Default chat model**: users on the *implicit* DeepInfra default now route to `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash` (Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B has been retired from the active catalog). Anyone who pinned a model explicitly via config is unaffected. - Backward compatible at the config layer; no schema changes, no env var renames, no migration script. ### Auth-context threading fix `provider-discovery.ts` now passes the resolved catalog context (`env`, `agentDir`, and `hasApiKey=true` once `buildSingleProviderApiKeyCatalog` has verified the key resolves) through to `discoverDeepInfraModels`. Without this, an auth-profile-only setup could authenticate at the discovery layer yet still receive the static catalog because `hasDeepInfraApiKey` had no `agentDir` to probe the profile store. Both env-var and auth-profile-only setups now receive the live 88-model catalog identically. ## Diagram ```text Before: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds 7 manifest entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: count > MODEL_CATALOG.length? -> skip discovery] -> [picker shows 7 models] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper -> provider !== "openrouter" -> short-circuit] -> [payload sent WITHOUT cache_control markers, prompt cache never fires] After: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: always run discovery] -> [discoverDeepInfraSurfaces: GET /v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta] -> [bucket by surface alias tag] -> [picker shows 88 chat + 38 vlm + ...] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper: model.id starts with "anthropic/" -> apply patch] -> [payload sent WITH cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}, prompt cache fires] ``` ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **Yes** — one read-only GET to `https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` per process per 5 minutes, gated on `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` being configured. The endpoint is unauthenticated; the gate is a UX choice (pre-auth flows show the static fallback, no useless network traffic). No PII or secrets in the request. - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Risks and Mitigations - **Risk**: production DeepInfra backend hasn't (yet) tagged any `video-gen` model with the OPENAI tag, so the `video-gen` surface bucket from live discovery is empty. **Mitigation**: the live catalog seam returns `null` on empty buckets so the static `DEEPINFRA_VIDEO_FALLBACK_MODELS` list wins. Once the backend tags T2V models, the live catalog takes over automatically — no deepclaw redeploy needed.
